title: Ctenitis chiapasensis
taxonomy: Ctenitis chiapasensis is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as polypody ferns. This species is further classified within the genus Ctenitis, which belongs to the order Polypodiales. Ctenitis chiapasensis is native to Mexico, specifically the state of Chiapas, from which it gets its name. The species is also sometimes classified as a member of the genus Dryopteris, although some sources consider Ctenitis to be a distinct genus. The classification of Ctenitis chiapasensis can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a species within the family Polypodiaceae.

intro: Ctenitis chiapasensis, also known as a fern species, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis chiapasensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
